title: Statement Related to Offer by AIG to Purchase Maiden Lane II LLC

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York issued a brief statement on March 11, 2011 acknowledging receipt of a formal offer from American International Group (AIG) to purchase assets held in Maiden Lane II LLC, a special purpose vehicle created during the 2008 financial crisis to facilitate AIG's unwind. The Fed stated it had been aware of AIG's interest and that any disposition decision would aim to maximize taxpayer proceeds and foster financial stability.
key_points: Federal Reserve received a formal offer from AIG to purchase Maiden Lane II LLC assets on March 11, 2011
The Fed stated it had been aware of AIG's interest in the MLII assets prior to the formal offer
The Fed committed to maximizing proceeds to the taxpayer in any disposition decision
The Fed stated disposition decisions would be consistent with fostering financial stability
Contact listed was Jack Gutt at the New York Fed (212) 720-6142
why_it_matters: This document relates to the wind-down of Maiden Lane II, a facility established during the 2008 financial crisis when the Fed extended credit to AIG. While historical in nature (2011), asset disposition from central bank crisis facilities can have market structure implications for understanding post-crisis resolution frameworks. However, this single-paragraph statement contains no specific details about the offer terms, asset values, or timeline.
market_structure_context: Maiden Lane II was established by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York in November 2008 as part of the AIG bailout. The 2008 annual report indicated MLII held approximately $24.3 billion in assets at inception. This statement indicates the Fed was actively working to wind down these crisis-era facilities and return assets to the private sector. However, the document provides no details on market structure implications of the disposition.
